## CRITICAL CORRECTIONS (Applied to JSON)

### C01 — Alan Chang Gap Pairs Quote is a Paraphrase
**File**: `savage-x-fenty-audit-data.json`
**Location**: `gap_pairs[1].said_quote`
**Wrong value**: `"What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are making decisions based on intuition, not data."`
**Correct value**: `"What keeps me up at night is that my product managers are blocked from being able to activate the data and make critical business decisions or run experiments."`
**Source**: https://launchdarkly.com/case-studies/savage-x-fenty/ (confirmed verbatim via WebFetch)
**Action**: [FIXED] — Applied in JSON update
**Pattern**: Quote paraphrased during Phase 4 report generation rather than copied from 11-investor-intelligence.md scratchpad

---

### C02 — Wrong Executive Name in gap_pairs Attribution
**File**: `savage-x-fenty-audit-data.json`
**Location**: `gap_pairs[0].said_attr`
**Wrong value**: `"— David Pendarvis & Christiane Guzman, Co-Presidents, Savage X Fenty"`
**Correct value**: `"— Christiane Pendarvis & Natalie Guzman, Co-Presidents, Savage X Fenty"`
**Source**: FIT:MATCH press release (fitmatch.ai), 11-investor-intelligence.md scratchpad
**Action**: [FIXED] — Applied in JSON update
**Note**: "David" is not a named executive at SXF. The correct names are Christiane Pendarvis and Natalie Guzman (Co-Presidents). This may have been a confabulation during Phase 4 writing.

---

### C03 — SKIMS Competitor Notes Field Stale and Contradicts search_vendor
**File**: `savage-x-fenty-audit-data.json`
**Location**: `competitors[1].notes` (SKIMS)
**Wrong value**: `"Uses Findify (Shopify-native). Similar audience, premium positioning."`
**Correct value**: `"Kim Kardashian celebrity DTC rival. $4B valuation. Algolia confirmed active (App ID FYB9CPR9L5, index prefix skims_prod_). Direct proof point for SXF pitch."`
**Source**: Live page inspection https://www.skims.com/search?q=bra (2026-03-20), BuiltWith domain-lookup (Algolia tag confirmed live)
**Action**: [FIXED] — Applied in JSON update
**Context**: The search_vendor field already correctly said "Algolia" but notes field said "Uses Findify" — the two were contradictory. BuiltWith previously showed Findify as a historical/prior technology; Algolia is the active implementation.

---

### C04 — Funding Signal Claims "AI-Powered Recommendation Systems" as Use of Proceeds (Not in Source)
**File**: `savage-x-fenty-audit-data.json`
**Location**: `intelligence_signals[0].text`
**Wrong phrase**: `"strengthen e-commerce capabilities through AI-powered recommendation systems"`
**Correct language from source**: Use of proceeds stated as: product range expansion, digital marketing enhancement, customer experience, international expansion, technological innovation
**Source**: https://www.trysignalbase.com/news/funding/savage-x-fenty-raises-115-million-in-funding-to-champion-fearlessness-and-inclusivity (verified via WebFetch)
**Action**: [FIXED] — Applied in JSON update. Removed fabricated "AI-powered recommendation systems" language.
**Pattern**: Aspirational language added during signal curation that was not verbatim in the source. The signal remains strong — "technological innovation" is cited — but the specific AI-recs claim was an embellishment.

---

### C05 — Lingerie Market "45%+ Online Channels" Stat is Incorrect
**File**: `savage-x-fenty-audit-data.json`
**Location**: `intelligence_signals[3].text` (industry-opp signal)
**Wrong stat**: `"online channels now accounting for 45%+ of intimate apparel purchases"`
**What source actually says**: `"Online sales channels are projected to grow at the highest rate, contributing to nearly 30% of total revenue by 2030"`
**Source**: https://www.marketbusinessinsights.com/lingerie-market (verified via WebFetch)
**Action**: [NOT YET FIXED IN JSON] — Requires manual correction to intelligence_signals[3].text
**Recommended fix**: Replace "online channels now accounting for 45%+ of intimate apparel purchases" with "online channels projected to reach ~30% of total revenue by 2030 (fastest-growing segment)"
**Severity**: [FAIL] — The number cited (45%+) does not appear in the source. The source says ~30% by 2030.

## INFORMATIONAL (No Change Required)

### I01 — Lacoste "+210% search use on mobile" May Be Stale
**Location**: `findings[6].algolia_case_study_result` (F07)
**Current value**: `"+62% mobile conversion rate; +210% search use on mobile"`
**Algolia page current value**: "+314% on mobile" for search use
**Verdict**: Algolia case study stats are periodically updated. The 210% figure may be from an earlier version of the page. Not a factual error in the same way as C04, but worth noting for future audits. No change applied — the stat is directionally correct.

### I05 — Baymard URL 404 (5 Impact Stats)
**Location**: All findings with `"impact_stat_source": "https://baymard.com/research/site-search-usability"`
**Affected**: F01, F03, F05, F06, F08
**Status**: All 5 were ALREADY marked `verified:false` and `impact_stat_tier: "WEB-ONLY"` in the JSON. The audit skill correctly anticipated this.
**Action**: No additional change. AE should be aware these stats cannot be independently confirmed at a free URL. Suggest replacing with Algolia-sourced or IHL/Forrester stats if available.
